channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 🏴 A US and allied strategy for Greenland | Atlantic Council
item.description: After US rhetoric about taking control of Greenland exacerbated tensions between Washington and European allies in January, the US, Greenland, and Denmark opened discussions about the US presence in the autonomous territory. In parallel, the Atlantic Council's Task Force on…

item.title: 🏛️ US Navy could run out of money by July, top officer warns | Defense News
item.description: Chief of Naval Operations Adm. Daryl Caudle told lawmakers at a budget hearing Tuesday that the Navy might have to modify training and operations.

item.title: 🌏 For Indo-Pacific states, the Arctic’s security importance is rising | ASPI Strategist
item.description: The Arctic is no longer a distant sideshow. It is becoming a contested strategic arena, and Indo-Pacific states should start treating it that way. What happens in the High North will increasingly affect global trade, ...

item.title: 🌏 House Foreign Affairs greenlights trio of arms sale bills, including Taiwan support | Breaking Defense
item.description: The committee tanked one bill, which would have allowed any country to use Foreign Military Financing to purchase weapons through the commercial sales process.

item.title: 📍 Contractor awarded $3.5 billion to build out US Coast Guard’s Arctic Security Cutters fleet | Defense News
item.description: The five cutters to be delivered by Davie Defense will represent a new class of Arctic icebreakers.

item.title: ⚛️ 10,000 Low-Cost Cruise Missiles In Three Years Procurement Plan Laid Out By Pentagon | The War Zone
item.description: The U.S. military now also plans on buying 12,000 'cheap' hypersonic missiles as part of a larger push to bolster munitions inventories. The post 10,000 Low-Cost Cruise Missiles In Three Years Procurement Plan Laid Out By Pentagon appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌏 The myth of US-China stability | Atlantic Council
item.description: Washington and Beijing do not trust each other. But expect Trump and Xi to remain on message this week, saying friendly things about each other. The post The myth of US-China stability appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🛡️ The US AI health data collision: Charting the future of US cross-border data flow policy, health data, and health and biopharma AI policy | Atlantic Council
item.description: The race to control America's most sensitive data is already underway, and most people have no idea. The post The US AI health data collision: Charting the future of US cross-border data flow policy, health data, and health and biopharma AI policy appeared first on Atlantic…

item.title: 🌍 UAE Building Massive ‘Cope Cages’ To Protect Energy Facilities From Iranian Drone Attacks | The War Zone
item.description: The move comes after the UAE has been hit by thousands of Iranian missiles and drones that have heavily damaged parts of its oil and gas infrastructure. The post UAE Building Massive ‘Cope Cages’ To Protect Energy Facilities From Iranian Drone Attacks appeared first on The War…

item.title: 🌏 There’s No Need to Fear China’s Economy | Foreign Policy
item.description: Beijing can’t easily afford to escalate any economic struggle with Washington.

item.title: 📍 L3Harris turns handheld radios into counter-drone jammers | Breaking Defense
item.description: The forthcoming Wraith Shield software update uses the existing antenna to scan for drone control signals, identify enemy ones, and jam them, L3Harris executives told reporters.

item.title: 🏴 US Army abruptly cancels deployment of 4,000 soldiers to Poland | Defense News
item.description: The Army has abruptly halted a planned deployment to Poland, bringing U.S. personnel numbers in Europe to pre-2022 levels — before Russia invaded Ukraine.

item.title: 🌍 Air Force MQ-9 fleet drops to 135 aircraft after Iran combat losses | Defense News
item.description: The Air Force is seeking to backfill the inventory of MQ-9 Reapers and field a cheaper, more expendable replacement.

item.title: 🏛️ Air Force expects new KC-46 vision system in 2028 as lawmakers question price hike | Breaking Defense
item.description: Sen. Kevin Cramer said lawmakers “need more information for sure” about projected price increases for the air refueler, which budget documents show could climb by $100 million per tail.

item.title: 🌏 Taiwan is a ‘model ally,’ not trade bait | Defense One
item.description: Trading the island’s security for rhetoric from Beijing would be a strategic blunder of historic proportions.

item.title: 🏛️ Why sustainment is the key to Marine Corps readiness, and how it’s changing | Breaking Defense
item.description: [Sponsored] From service-life upgrades to safety enhancements, readiness depends on sustaining the vehicles now carrying the mission.

item.title: ⚛️ 7,800 Interceptors In Space At Core Of $1.2 Trillion Golden Dome Cost Estimate | The War Zone
item.description: This massive undertaking in low Earth orbit would still only be able to engage 10 ICBMs simultaneously, according to the CBO. The post 7,800 Interceptors In Space At Core Of $1.2 Trillion Golden Dome Cost Estimate appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 📍 Analyzing Trump’s Foreign Policy and Its International Implications | War on the Rocks
item.description: In 2016, Ben Friedman wrote, “The Trump Administration Will be Hawkish,” where he argued that despite Trump’s non-interventionist campaign rhetoric, structural forces, hawkish appointees, and an entrenched foreign policy bureaucracy would push him closer toward conventional…

item.title: 🏛️ After Middle East ops, Navy to start feeling funding crunch this summer: CNO | Breaking Defense
item.description: The aircraft carrier Gerald R. Ford is expected to face increased maintenance due to its extended deployment.

item.title: 🌏 Role of Indo-Pacific air defenders has evolved dramatically, US Army commander says | Defense News
item.description: Brig. Gen. William Parker, 94th AAMDC chief, recalled an old air defense building bearing a "Will work for food" sign, a stark contrast to the role today.

item.title: 🌍 Why Congress Can’t Stop Trump’s Iran War | Foreign Policy
item.description: A half-century of messy politics has inverted the Constitution’s design—and there’s no easy fix.

item.title: 🕵️ West Pointers can be trained to better evaluate, appreciate AI, study finds | Defense One
item.description: New research may point the way to harnessing AI’s potential on the battlefield—and in society.

item.title: 🏛️ What Don Bacon has sizzling before he leaves office | Breaking Defense
item.description: The departing lawmaker and former Air Force officer still has a few things on his to-do list before the end of the 119th Congress.

item.title: 🌍 Israel Building Factory To Pump Out Its Own FPV Drones | The War Zone
item.description: The IDF is racing to catch up to Hezbollah's increasing use of these weapons, which has taken a grim toll on its forces in Lebanon. The post Israel Building Factory To Pump Out Its Own FPV Drones appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: ⚛️ Pentagon launches new framework agreements to acquire 10,000 low-cost cruise missiles | Breaking Defense
item.description: The agreements with Anduril, CoAspire, Leidos and Zone 5 commence the launch of the new Low Cost Containerization Munitions Program (LCCMP).

item.title: 🌍 Is It Really a ‘Cease-Fire’ if Both Sides Are Still Shooting? | Foreign Policy
item.description: A mediation expert explains why the U.S.-Iran cease-fire is so vulnerable.

item.title: 🏛️ Air Force greenlights requirements for MQ-9A Reaper drone replacement | Breaking Defense
item.description: “There is, I think, a burgeoning interest across the broader defense industrial base on what comes next,” said Air Force Lt. Gen. Luke Cropsey.

item.title: 🏴 Russia and the China-US Summit | The Diplomat
item.description: The upcoming meeting between U.S. President Donald Trump and Xi will be followed by a Putin-Xi summit. Will there be scope for triangular diplomacy?

item.title: 📍 Working ‘outside the lines’ to scale production isn’t working: Cadenazzi | Breaking Defense
item.description: DIU still vets drone tech, but a new strategy says wartime scaling requires internal attention and reform from others.

item.title: ⚛️ Pentagon reaches agreements with defense firms on containerized missiles | Defense News
item.description: The Pentagon is announcing framework agreements with Anduril, CoAspire, Leidos and Zone 5 to acquire over 10,000 containerized missiles.
